title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 August 2024.

items.title: **Red Dead Redemption**'s US PlayStation page talks about the long awaited (but not confirmed) PC version
bullets: "Experience the epic western adventures that defined a generation — now on PC for the first time ever. Also included is Undead Nightmare, the iconic horror story that transforms the world of Red Dead Redemption into an apocalyptic fight for survival against a zombie horde. Featuring the complete single-player experiences of both games, including bonus content from the Game of the Year Edition, Red Dead Redemption features all of the 2023 console version upgrades plus PC specific enhancements - including support for increased resolutions and framerates, multiple displays, and other accessories, plus spatial surround sound.*

items.title: A new **Sunsoft** retro compilation brings three Famicom games to the west for the first time
bullets: "Sunsoft is Back! Retro Games Collection" will cost $9.99 digitally
The three games included are Firework Thrower Kantaro’s 53 Stations of the Tokaido, The Wing of Madoola and Ripple Island.

items.title: A **Halloween** (as in the film series not Halloween the time of year) game is in development in UE5, from the creators of Evil Dead: The Game with John Carpenter contributing
bullets: In a statement Carpenter, who’s said to be “intimately involved” with the game’s development, said: “As a huge gamer myself, I’m thrilled to help bring Michael Myers to life again in this game, and my hope is to scare you silly.”

items.title: Open-world game **Terminator: Survivors** has been delayed to 2025
bullets: Previously scheduled to hit PC on October 24, 2024, before coming to consoles at a later date, the game will now be released in 2025.
Terminator: Survivors is set in the world of the first two Terminator films, and will follow an original story which takes place after the second movie, four years following Judgment Day.
